results.possibleIdentity: Harold R. Ellis was a New York-based electrical engineer, scientific journalist, and editor whose career spanned several decades, starting in the mid-1940s. He graduated from the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, in 1949 with a degree in electrical engineering, during which time he was active in the American Institute of Electrical Engineers (A.I.E.E.) and served as an editor for the student publication, The Tennessee Engineer. Ellis established a career in technical journalism in New York City, serving as the Technical Editor and Associate Editor for the trade magazine Radio Retailing (and its successor, Radio & Television Retailing) during the late 1940s. His professional focus was notably interdisciplinary, covering fields such as electrical engineering, psychology, cybernetics, control theory, and communications. He was associated with the nonprofit organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), which promoted collaborations between the arts and the technical sciences. Later in his career, he transitioned into psychology and dream research, holding an M.Ed. and serving as the coordinator of the Dreamsharing Grassroots Network in New York. He authored articles on "Dream Drama" and was a prominent figure in the Dream Educators Network during the 1970s and 1990s. He operated professionally as a consultant under the name Harold R. Ellis & Associates.
